audit_scope: 5 newly authored agent pages · template-mass-produced · template-level findings prioritised
dimensions_note: Tone-of-voice climbed from 80 (last audit) to 95 — banned-vocab sweep clean across all 5; source-doc refs sweep clean across all 5; no §Sources blocks on any page. Honesty register held back by ai-cataloging live-deployment overclaim (H1) and trend-to-design status calibration (M1). Cross-page held back by P7 nav-wiring partial state (mega-menu added but home-page card unchanged from 'AI Agents · catalog' generic strapline).
template_level_findings: All 5 pages share the same 6-section structure (Hero · Job · Workflow SVG · Data · Patterns · Evals · Linked Platforms) — uniform shape across the catalog ✓
All 5 SVG diagrams use the cortex-planning template (Inputs blue / Workflow grey / Outputs green, trunk fan-in for inputs) — visual consistency ✓
All 5 pages source-doc-ref clean — zero Accenture / Farooq / Agentisation mentions in body copy ✓
All 5 pages banned-vocab clean — zero leverages / unlocks / empowers / transformative / etc ✓

findings.section: ai-cataloging · §03 Inputs zone + §07 Publish to channels + Status pill
findings.issue: ai-cataloging.yaml claims 'Live deployments: TIRA · JioMart · AJIO · Reliance Trends' on the page (input zone caption + step 07 detail). Source compilation backs only TIRA (external eval PDF cites TIRA explicitly; internal Eval PDF is methodology). JioMart, AJIO, Reliance Trends are *channel surfaces the agent can publish to*, not confirmed live deployments. Page reads as 4 live deployments; source supports 1.
findings.fix: Edit data/agents/ai-cataloging.yaml — change the 'Live deployments' caption to 'Live: TIRA. Building: JioMart, AJIO, Reliance Trends.' Or drop the channel list entirely and let the §07 Publish step list channels as targets without implying live status.

findings.issue: category-intelligence.yaml linked_platforms includes a card pointing to /agents/trend-to-design/ (another agent), not a platform. The §06 contract is 'platforms the agent runs on / depends on' — agent-to-agent navigation has no surface in the current template. Reader clicking 'Trend-to-Design' lands on another agent detail page expecting a platform.
findings.fix: Drop the Trend-to-Design entry from category-intelligence linked_platforms. The downstream relationship can be expressed inline in §02 Workflow steps ('outputs feed Trend-to-Design') without a card. Same applies in reverse for any future agent.

findings.issue: trend-to-design status pill is 'Building' but hero stats announce '718 designs in <2 days' and '~60% selection rate' — past-tense outcomes that read as live, not building. Mixed signal: pill says we haven't shipped, body says we shipped 718 and got 60% selection.
findings.fix: Either flip status to 'Live' (the Trend business run is a real shipped deployment) or qualify the hero stat with 'Pilot run · trend business · Apr 2026' to clarify that's a single run, not a steady-state production. User judgement call — recommend Live since the run was production-equivalent.

findings.issue: Hero tile 'Cycle compression: Hours · context: vs weeks today · 11 teams · 6 manual handoffs' reads as a marketing-deck before/after rather than a verifiable number. Tone-of-voice §5 stat tile formula expects a number that proves it works. 'Hours' is an aspiration, not a measured outcome.
findings.fix: Replace 'Hours' with a verifiable number once the first end-to-end campaign runs (e.g., '4 hr · pilot Diwali campaign'). For now, drop the tile or replace with 'Cycle target: hours · in-development' to make the aspirational nature explicit.

findings.issue: retail-vista has only 1 linked_platforms card (just /retail-vista/ itself · 'Platform home'). This reads as the §08 section being incomplete since other agents have 2-3 cards. Per spec §3 §08 the section is 'card grid · 1-3 cards per agent' — 1 is allowed but feels sparse. Retail Vista's outputs feed Cortex (network expansion plans) and JCP (delivery-route optimisation in some framings); both could be card targets.
findings.fix: Add downstream platform consumers — at minimum '/impetus/cortex/' (consumes spatial context for store-level planning). Optional '/jcp/' (delivery routing / catchment). Source: Retail Vista feeds spatial context to multiple downstream platforms per the writeup.

findings.issue: Evals status field reads 'in-development · framework defined' but the page renders this as 'GATES AND THRESHOLDS' followed by 3 forward-looking gates with no current measurement. No baseline number; no current pass rate. Reader can't tell if anything is being measured today.
findings.fix: Add a one-line current-state qualifier under the gates list: 'Current: framework documented; first end-to-end campaign in setup' or similar. Same recommendation applies anywhere evals.status is 'in-development'.

findings.issue: Home page (index.html) /agents/ card metric reads '17 agents · Customer/Plan/Buy/Move/Sell/HR' — stale. Catalog now has 6 agents in 3 tracks (Plan/Buy/Sell). Apex reader on home page sees 17, lands on /agents/ and sees 6.
findings.fix: Edit index.html line ~194 — change metric to '6 agents · Plan/Buy/Sell' and desc to drop the 17-agent enumeration. Match the live catalog state.

findings.issue: /agents/ index page hero subhead reads 'Six agents shipping in v1' — uses spelled-out 'Six' while the rest of the register uses Arabic numerals consistently (per tone-of-voice §4: 'Percentage as 83% not eighty-three percent' — same principle for counts).

Hero stat 'Live trend run · 718 designs · context: 10 brands · under 2 days · autonomous'. Word 'autonomous' is fine but 'under 2 days' uses prose where '<2 days' or '~2 days' would match the register's mono-stat brevity.
findings.fix: Edit data/agents/trend-to-design.yaml stats[0].context — '10 brands · under 2 days · autonomous' → '10 brands · ~2 days · autonomous'.

findings.section: All 5 pages · workflow SVG arrow snake
findings.issue: The trunk-pattern arrow into S1 (5 inputs converging) renders cleanly on cortex-planning but on the new pages where the inputs zone has 4 sources + 1 human gate, the trunk vertical bar at x=275 is correctly drawn but very short trunk segments (just 31px from input box to bar) read as 'connector stubs' rather than a clear collection bus.
findings.fix: Visually OK at desktop; revisit if mobile rendering shows clipping. No change for now.

findings.issue: Footer line reads 'Owner · Salman Saudagar (COO, Fynd) · v0.8.4' — site-wide pre-existing per v1 audit M1. New pages inherit. Per canonical convention should be copyright-only.
findings.fix: Out of scope (separate site-wide cleanup task).

strengths: Banned-vocab and source-doc-ref sweep clean across all 5 pages — zero hits on transformative / leverages / Accenture / Farooq / etc. The new tone-of-voice §3 rules are landing.

All 5 pages render the identical 6-section template (§01 Job · §02 Workflow · §03 Data · §04 Patterns · §05 Evals · §06 Linked Platforms) — Apex reader can compare across the catalog without per-page surprises.

All 5 SVG diagrams follow the same Inputs (blue) → Workflow (grey) → Outputs (green) zone pattern with the trunk-fan-in convention. Visual consistency across the catalog is the load-bearing differentiator.

All 5 hero stat tiles trace to source artefacts in the compilation folder — '718 designs' (AI Design Agent Note), '11 agents' (Marketing Multi-Agent Framework), '15 days → 5 hr' (Cataloging Eval PDF), '8 categories' (trend-engine repo), '61 datasets' (RetailVista Business Overview).
The 'Detail expanding · ETA' pattern from cortex-planning's note_pending was not copy-pasted into the new pages — no thin-source agent got authored here. Discipline held.
blockers_to_ship: H1 · ai-cataloging live-deployment overclaim (TIRA only sourced)
M1 · trend-to-design status vs hero stats mismatch (user judgement)
